page: Microsoft Azure D2pls_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance belonging to the DPLSv5 server family. It is powered by an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor based on the ARMv8 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The instance features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports hardware-accelerated AES cryptography, though it lacks advanced vector and matrix extensions such as AVX or AMX. The instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the D2pls_v5 is positioned in the lower tier for resource-intensive workloads. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance is average at 1822.08, while multi-core performance falls into the weak category at 3617.67. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 140.47 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 14.05 GB/sec, both rated as poor. Similarly, Geekbench scores reflect limited computational capacity, with a single-core score of 1072.0 and a multi-core score of 1903.0. Single-threaded gzip compression performs at an average rate of 24.13 GB/sec, but multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation tasks show weaker throughput.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D2pls_v5 offers a low-resource, dedicated ARM hardware profile suitable for entry-level workloads where high memory capacity and intensive floating-point computations are not required. Given the lack of bundled local storage and the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic text processing, simple database operations, and small-scale application hosting. It can also support lightweight development environments and basic containerized microservices that do not demand high memory bandwidth or advanced vector instructions.
description: Microsoft Azure D2pls_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance from the DPLSv5 family, utilizing a 3.0 GHz Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs. It features 4.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a low-memory 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and complimentary public IPv4 addresses but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression, alongside weaker multi-core capabilities and poor memory bandwidth. Lacking advanced vector extensions like AVX or AMX, the D2pls_v5 is a qualitatively cost-efficient option for lightweight workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, simple database operations, text processing, and small-scale containerized microservices.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2pls_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring a 3.0 GHz Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor, 2 dedicated vCPUs, and 4.0 GB of memory, designed for lightweight, low-memory workloads.

tagline: A low-memory, dedicated ARM-based virtual machine designed for lightweight web serving and entry-level application hosting.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 processor
Features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage
Average single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Best suited for lightweight web serving and basic database operations
